channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 200 replies — 800.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 200 • Updated: 2026-07-20 07:19 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496570
>>62496401
Ok Nico.

62496684
>>62496520
Do you really think getting into a semantic debate with DTCC's wording is going to make you feel better about XRP not being involved at all?

62496690
Okay they've been live for a week, where's my $1000 LINK? Or even just my $100 LINK? Or perhaps even just my $10 LINK?

62496743
>>62496684
I don't invest in any crypto whatsoever. Every inch of it is for retards.

62496998
>>62496743
>Every inch of it is for retards.
Yeah, retards like DTCC, Swift, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, Nasdaq, NYSE, S&P Dow Jones Indices, the US Department of Commerce, and many many more.

62497078
>>62496544
You’re arguing against fractionalization, not tokenization. Fractional ownership has existed for decades through REITs, ETFs, ABS, and syndicated loans. Tokenization isn’t claiming to invent that. It’s an attempt to modernize the ownership and settlement rails. Whether it succeeds is an empirical question, not one answered by pointing out that fractionalization already exists. Many projects are hype, but that doesn’t make the underlying infrastructure worthless.

62497218
>>62496998
>Yeah, retards like DTCC, Swift, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, Nasdaq, NYSE, S&P Dow Jones Indices, the US Department of Commerce, and many many more.
Sure sure. They've all "expressed interest" or "have a team", but none of them are actually using it. It's just lip service to keep their fingers in many pies. I'll say it again, this is 100% useless, is going nowhere and is being used ONLY as star trek gibberish to keep greater fools on the hook. Using a public ledger (which is all crypto brings to the table) to do any of these things is utterly pointless when it can all be done via traditional digital systems at about 100000x the speed.

62497425
>>62497218
Wall of cope

62497444
>>62497425
p+c

62497473
>>62497218
/thread
link retards always resort to muh appeal to authority

62497528
>>62497218
>>62497473
settle down verbo, surely XRP will be involved next time (lol)

62497544
>>62497528
p+c

62497604
>>62497528
>61 pbtid
>"settle down"
lel the irony

62497795
>>62497528
Sslinkie1 is at it again

62497824
>>62497473
two paths for a crypto
>be full privacy and cyberpunk
>be full authority approved

62498098
>>62497824
2 paths for a link shill
>keep spouting wordsalad buzzword carrot dangles for the next decade
>admit failure and rope

62498297
>>62497824
third option:
>p+c

62498300
>>62498098
wordsalad: "the tech is ready"
realsalad: Chainlink advised SEC on crypto, law makers on Clarity Act
realsalad: Chainlink partner with DTCC, Swift and other institutions
>>62498297
it's going up like usual

62498304
>>62497604
>>62497795
>>62498297
xerpies absolutely seething

62498366
>>62498300
I see you decided on option b

62498376
>>62498366
the buy link option

62498552
>>62498304
>>62498304
you still posting screenshots of your tweets on biz to try and get followers?

62499092
>>62498552
You mean like Adem/Thomas the xerpie has been doing for half a decade?

62499460
>>62496397
Not sure which chart you're looking at...

62501067
>>62466449

https://youtu.be/qAIrj_Vqdfc

62501106
>>62501019
>we know it works. we just don't know what it'll be used for, or how to make it useful in a legal sense, or how to make it work widely enough to be useful.
lol. You guys should start watching David Ike videos. The man can talk for 8 hours nonstop about absolutely nothing, and sound convincing. He's especially gifted at it. These crypto grifters are just doing it to you instead of theaters full of boomers.

62501441
>>62501106
>we just don't know what it'll be used for, or how to make it useful in a legal sense, or how to make it work widely enough to be useful.
She's saying the opposite of all this.

You just don't like what she's saying because it rules out XRP.

62501468
>>62501441
nigger I don't even know what XRP really is. I have no ties to crypto whatsoever. I would never touch anything that specifically appeals to fools who like to be made to feel smart.

62501476
>>62501468
>specifically appeals to fools who like to be made to feel smart
Fools like DTCC and Swift?

item.title: AirFox — 62 replies — 134.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
AirFox

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-07-20 06:54 UTC

OP

Any qrd on what’s going on with this thing? What exactly are these guys hoping to accomplish? Crypto is cooked and they keep trying to change this project in ways that don’t seem to matter. The TG link also doesn’t work anymore.

Early replies (first 3)

62481067
>>62480640
>2 hours later
>0 replies
jfc it’s worse than I thought. These threads used to reach bump limits after a couple hours.

62481088
>>62480640
it's a dead shitcoin, the devs are just waiting for the few final bagholders to give up so they can wrap it up and enjoy the money they made selling this shit

62481280
>>62480640
its just a rebrand and a relaunch with shifty eyes about robinhood instead of base.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62487573
>>62486525
a few bags were worth 7 figs at the height (yeah yeah, muh liquidity, I know) silly fuckers held all this time, that's gotta hurt, a few got out with 6 figs but fucked the chart

62487601
>>62486284
whoa, wtf? hadn't even spotted that, 9k now.....pffffffhahahahah

62487664
>>62487100
Solana is part of why crypto got fucked imo. Outside of the scammers and a few degens, I can’t imagine too many people making money there. But yea I don’t see any light at the end of the tunnel for crypto as a whole

62489045
Damn we hit $200k. Which one of you fucks is selling?

62489432
>>62481067
If you haven't noticed, anon, jannies and jeets wrung every drop of juice out of this board for years. I still come back on occasion and reply out of habit, just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread. /biz/ is a fucking ghost town

62489758
Wait a minute tho, if he does actually launch this token launcher etc for robin hood, it could be one of the early launches and go to the moon

62489887
>>62489432
>just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread
lol I thought it was just me. I’ll go into what seems like an active thread and as soon as I post a reply, that shit dies. It’s weird

>>62489758
>robinhood
I’m very skeptical about sentiment going back to what it was. I honestly don’t think crypto will ever be a space where we see 100x’s across the board. Even 2024s run was shit and I don’t think we will see that either. I see robinhood as another Base type of thing. Granted, Base did pretty good in the beginning but it ultimately turned out to be a chain for insider trading. That’s why crypto is in the state that it’s in. Nothing but a bunch of jews

62489964
>>62489045
>down 27% off less than 4k volume
yeah this shit is cooked.

62490304
>>62489887
>I see robinhood as another Base type of thing
I don’t even see it as that. Remember how Base was supposed to onboard 1b people? Remember how bad the on chain summer was? Coinbase was, and still is, one of the most well known names in the space and they couldn’t even pull it off. People stopped caring. I hope I’m wrong but all the things are leading to RH being another nothing burger

62490407
>>62487664
Yeah Scamlana fucked the shitcoin market and stole too much liquidity. And before that, a new useless ghost chain copy pasted version of ETH being created every week.
it's ogre for ETH and the shitcoin market overall
>T. Roundtripped a $110k Apu bag and held like a good baggie

62490814
>>62490407
>roundtripped
That’s the thing that worries me if we ever did have another bull run. The dumps were just as quick as the pumps and a lot of people were caught off guard. Too many baggies were created and many are still holding on, including whales. I see that across a lot of the stuff I’m in. So if we ever did have a run up I feel like the sell pressure would be worse than before because everyone that’s still holding will want to exit with whatever profits they can get. All the shitcoins with little liquidity are going to get nuked because of how pvp it will get. I’m a baggie who round tripped a mid-upper 6 figure bag across a bunch of projects and I can tell you right now that my standards of what I consider to be “making it” is far less than before. idgaf about making 7 figures anymore. Even getting 1/3 of my ATH is enough for me to exit and I’m sure a lot of people feel this way

62490948
>>62489045
>Which one of you fucks is selling?
lmao, you must be new here

62491616
I just noticed that 60m wallet that’s selling is an early wallet that has ties to paw / shib related stuff. I’m getting ‘frens and supporters’ vibes with this one. It’s likely the team knows who owns that wallet. It’s never going to end…..

62493068
and with that…avi’s story has come to a close. happy third birthday aviator. the final day . i am done. goodbye. 3 years too long. it’s not cozy anymore its just sad. i just cant do this anymore im sorry

62493393
>>62493068
>im out
The last sells were $48 and $4. That you?

62493444
>>62493393
no its not even worth it for me to go through the process of selling, im just done with the tg and having any hope at all for the team. its obvious they cannot find success in anything they make. sure they made stuff that works but nobody cares outside of 3 active users in tg. nothing can save this coin or the products, nobody cares. nobody uses the arcade, nobody uses the bridge. they fumbled every step of the way and it’s too far gone. stixil is vibe coding the stratus and it looks like everyone else left? we need a wellness check on pengo

62493696
>>62493444
I don’t know what is left of the team. It would be nice to at least know that much.
>no one cares
I agree but what narratives are left that people care about? It’s all been done before. It’s not even an issue with avi imo it’s the whole market. Most of the volume out there is fake. Things that seem to be doing good are really not. Wash trading and other manipulative tactics are used to make it look like crypto is alive. I believe stixil genuinely tried to make a legit project. Crypto is a tough space to build in and be successful. The ex-shib narrative was really the only thing they had going for it hem in regards to connections but ultimately it didn’t do anything.

62494825
>>62480640
I told you THIS SHIT WAS GOING TO GO TO ZERO.
WHY DID YOU NOT SELL? WHY?

62494980
WTFWT

62495070
>>62494825
I bought at $300k and my $100 has nearly doubled since. Cope and seethe tranny

62495627
make the price go up now. stixil you owe me that much atleast. let me exit with my pride.

62495664
>>62495627
The price is 2x from where it was yesterday. This is your last chance. Don’t get caught round tripping again

62497002
>>62495664
Check the price. The early friends and supporters strike again KEKW!

62497279
Idk what this coin does but I like cute anthro foxes. This is a fox business board.

62497771
>>62480640
I honestly don’t even know what’s happening with it. I bought a little over $100 worth over the past year and I can hardly get an accurate read on its present value. Am I just a boomer or did I get scammed (or both)?

62497947
>>62497771
they pulled the liquidity but they didn't run so that's a good sign on it not being a scam, they did it by doing an airdrop. They say they are still solvent but that just means that in conjunction with pulling the liquidity shows that avf is on probably running on fumes and doesn't have much operational budget left, Unless it can see some success. That said I think the arcade beta is actually really good.

Stixil's problem is that he care's too much about the chart and not enough about the business and makes some decisions to help the chart, that end up hurting the business.

I'm cautiously optimistic. Optimistic since I do think the arcade is a good idea. but i'm also cautious because of having to reassure solvency just means its something that stixil thinks that people would be worried about, which means its something that he might be worried about, which means we're running out of runway.

62497950
Stixil is playing games with my money now. either make the price go up or give me my refund. nothing you promised me happened, where is my cent per avi? you promised me my cent and now it’s time to pay up. who is going to buy my coins now? how about you give all of your holders USDC at value of their AVI holdings and be done with this little circus? it’s time to pay up your time has come to pay us

62498053
>>62497947
It really does feel like it’s running on fumes at this point. Realistically, what else is there left for stixil to do? V1, V2, the arcade, the audits, all these things were supposed to be yuge. Now we have to wait for stratus to launch in Q3 but what’s the point. They will make an x post announcing the launch of stratus and only 10 people will care. Then what? Just sit around hoping that people get interested if the market recovers? Everything avi is trying to do has already been done for the most part.

62498181
Personal opinion, for me celebrating 3 years by rugging holders isnt the best look. A 60m+ holder dumped his bag, which i assume is due to this action or some falling out. Signaling they are out of funds (red flag) then requesting users that already got rug pulled via liquidity grab to then put in more money for liquidity (another red flag). Perhaps all i see is red flags and there are some green flags but its not looking good

62501458
>>62498053
The purpose of producing some kind of product like this is to get some exchange to adopt it, no way in hell normies are going to care about skybridge

I've already accepted that i burned my money

item.title: So how much can we expect for the next BTC bullrun? — 28 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
So how much can we expect for the next BTC bullrun?

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-07-20 06:43 UTC

OP

So how much can we expect for the next BTC bullrun? Could we get a 3x at least?

Early replies (first 3)

62488594
>>62488572
110k top

62488607
Doubling time will double unless it turns into a double sigmoid.

62488619
>>62488572
I don't like my girl barefoot because then they're dirty and I can't lick them at a moments notice

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62488660
>>62488572
It depends on rates and liquidity, something /biz/ constantly ignores, even though it's the main catalyst for the price of Bitcoin.

62488665
It will be a 5x
From 20k to 100k
No ATH will be broken next run.

62488784
>>62488572
$1,000,000 at least

62491087
>>62488572
How does 250k sound?

62491165
>>62488572
5% under saylor avg price.

62491396
>>62488572
No, BTC is going literally to zero. People will laugh and the industry will be humiliated but we eventually recover without BTC. I'm talking completely stalled chain and delisted from every exchange.

62491405
>>62491087
Was thinking close to this memed up

62491425
Epstein pedo coin fee machine

62491426
>>62488572
Depends on if the banana peel pattern finally breaks out (blue lines)
If not, the top will be about 145k in 2028. However, eventually these lines will converge. It’s just a matter of whether it breaks out this cycle or next.
Oh and bottom is in by the way.

62491754
>>62491426
US and global semiconductors are imploding, GOOG rugged 4% today, and we haven’t even invaded Iran yet, and it’s not even September yet, and it’s not even midterms yet, and Trump is making it clear he’s going to declare an emergency to make sure Republicans win midterms.


Ahh…


So in what world do you see us appreciating in the next few months?

62496031
>>62488572
3X is very possible
>>62491396
No its not, we can see more trend around lending with TBV integrating with AAVE, BTC will hit 200K

62496038
>>62488665
This, but $80k top in 2029.
Law of diminishing return + dome theory.

62496054
Why would anyone buy crypto it’s all scams and meme coins now

Dead tech no one wants

62496175
>>62488572
At 21 she hates the idea of getting pregnant
In 20 years she'll be seething online that her divorced ex won't get back with her and she wants to make a test tube baby from her frozen eggs

62497340
>>62491396
Trash like BSV, BCH, ETC, and many other shitcoins are still alive, even though no one, even the worst shills, believes in them anymore. BTC is widely used as a store of wealth and means of barter, from nation states to corporations, to banks, and to wealthy individuals.
I'm not even a maxi, I believe there will eventually be a flippening, but it doesn't mean BTC will die.

62497349
>>62496031
>Could we get a 3x at least?

Sure from the EOY bottom at 20k!!!

62497610
>>62488572
>the next BTC bullrun

62497625
ONE HUNDRED AND SEVENTY FIVE THOUSAND DOLLARIDOOS JUST DO NOT ADJUST FOR INFLATION

62497653
>war in Iran escalates to the point where oil infrastructure is destroyed to the point where it will take years to rebuild
>GCC countries stop buying US bonds
>The fed is forced to print and buy its own debt
>hyperinflation begins, everyone goes to the bank to take out their money, the whole system collapses
>both PM and BTC do a 100x by the end of the year as they absorb wealth running away from the fiat collapse

It's literally your last chance to buy

62497982
>>62497653
>both PM and BTC do a 100x by the end of the year as they absorb wealth running away from the fiat collapse
you guys never learn

62498222
>>62497653
They'll do 100x but the USD will be worth 1000x less. You'll have to bring wheelbarrows of cash to afford a single mcdonalds hashbrown.

62498473
>>62491754
>So in what world do you see us appreciating in the next few months?
lol isn't this supposedly the exact scenario where bitcoin is supposed to shine? When the dollar and the economy is precarious? I swear, cryptards are just the biggest rubes there have ever been.

62500829
>>62496175
> and she wants to make a test tube baby from her frozen eggs
Nah, most of them are too lazy to go through with freezing an egg until it's too late.

62500937
>>62500829
They only thing I can learn from woman is massive hate and jealousy and the desire to abortion no matter how high their education is.

62501443
>>62488572
95k top, diminishing highs from here on out for next decade

item.title: Japan is overtaking TSMC right now — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Japan is overtaking TSMC right now

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-20 07:21 UTC

OP

I own property near Chitose
You jelly?
https://semiwiki.com/forum/threads/japan’s-rapidus-takes-on-tsmc-with-aggressive-2nm-pricing.25489/

Early replies (first 3)

62500426
>>62500225
Just tell me how to invest, fag.

62500436
>>62500225
Japanese products is so expensive just like Apple build in the USA. It is not sustainable, but who care.

62500443
>>62500225
finally

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62501153
>>62500225
are you chinese? they own a lot of land in Hokkaido, much to the chagrin of the locals

62501156
>>62501153
>ID: 3uj0WwwW
>草不可避WWW

62501225
How do I invest in rapidus before it goes to 2 trillion?

62501231
>>62500225
SELL SELL SELL SELL

62501233
The Nintendo of AI.

62501283
>>62500225
isnt east asia very seismic? why do they keep building fabs over there.

62501340
land has been selected. that means 2nm chips will be produced in

>a fortnight

62501477
Tsmc has atleast 30x rapidus capacity

item.title: I'll let you in on a secret I learned from working in elder finance. — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'll let you in on a secret I learned from working in elder finance.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-20 07:17 UTC

OP

I'll let you in on a secret I learned from working in elder finance. Literally every old person gets scammed out of their money to some extent, either by exorbitant nursing homes, selfish relatives, or just outright scams and frivolous lawsuits. Unless you have kids to protect you, your money WILL be taken from you once you're old. The world sees old people as resources to be harvested.

Early replies (first 3)

62500373
>>62500368
my bipolar elderly roastie (divorced, kids never visited her, alcoholic, didn't take her meds, etc.) was getting scammed by like 3 phone bills at once.

it had a happy ending when i called the police on her after a bipolar episode, and her brother (who she insisted was going to killer her) moved her in to some sort of assisted living facility.

62500375
>>62500373
elderly roastie NEIGHBOR

62500442
>>62500368
I'm not a fucking retard living in 1990 like my parents, I keep on top of trends and technology.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62500619
>>62500368
Everyone is getting scammed to some extent. What do you think inflation is doing to you? Government, banks, and their cronies stealing your money right out from under your nose.

62500684
>>62500368
Yea, they are the fat aphids in an ant nest waiting to be drained of their last drop of sap before their bodies are consumed when they get too old to eat.

62500950
>>62500368
>The world sees old people as resources to be harvested.
>Licking my lips at the thought....

62501265
>>62500368
You own something only under certain circumstances.

62501281
It doesn't matter because I won't have kids and my mom has said she's gonna spend all her money.

I got 'free' college which never helped me at all because no one cares if you have a degree. That was my 'inheritance'.

62501282
>>62500373
>>62500375
thought it was your mom lol

>>62500950
I've thought about doing another 4 years in college to become a geriatric doctor harvesting 90yo boomers for cash. That's 30 years of market right there.

62501402
>>62500950
You can be a elderly caregiver. My friend works as one, and it's drain all your energy.

62501473
So wr should booby trap the money is what youe saying.

item.title: I won my NEETbux court case and got $90K of back pay deposited into my bank account. — 28 replies — 29.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I won my NEETbux court case and got $90K of back pay deposited into my bank account.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-07-20 06:23 UTC

OP

I won my NEETbux court case and got $90K of back pay deposited into my bank account. I now get 1700 per month, no taxes.

What should I do with my money? Someone told me I should movie to flyover country like Ohio and buy a fourplex with an FHA mortgage, so I can live in one unit and collect rent from the other three. Note that SSDI has no means testing for income or assets. And I can collect it outside the country.

Thoughts?
>hardmode: no crypto

Early replies (first 3)

62498475
>>62498458
fake story

62498477
you could literally get your dick sucked off a story like that

62498481
>>62498458
Not how that works. Fake retarded story.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62498484
>>62498475
>>62498481
How about you point out what you think is fake instead of just calling it fake?
I had to go through years of denial and appeals to get it. I had to hire a lawyer.

62498494
>>62498484
The he would have said not to disclose the settlement amount. There is no recorded settlement matching your fake story.

62498508
>>62498494
It isn’t a settlement; I won on appeal. Besides, it is an administrative process, not a tort action. All of it is not releasable by the government due to the Privacy Act. I have no restriction.
Please take law 101 before trying to sound intelligent.

62498510
>>62498508
Got any proof?

62498522
>>62498510
Of course I do. Award letter, SSA-Treasury deposits in my bank account, etc. but I’m not gonna give anyone shit. That isn’t the purpose of this thread.

62498533
>>62498522
Serious question, if you're well-versed in the law, and I assume you possess above average intelligence, why do you collect welfare?

62498537
>>62498533
>why does someone who is determined to be disabled collect disability insurance benefits?
It’s a mystery.

62498565
>>62498533
He basically came here for attention.

62498586
>>62498458
how did u do it? I think i have a good chance since ive been admitted over 5 times to psych wards.. i lost my last job due to mental illness literally went insane and said some shit to my boss which probably made him hate me

62498622
>>62498458
>>62498484
>>62498508
>>62498522
>>62498537
You can't get "back pay" unless you have a job that you worked and didn't get paid for. The only part of this story that is true is that you are a NEET.

62498782
>>62498586
Just read online about how to gather your records and apply. Have some money set aside for experts. Retain an attorney when they deny you.
>>62498622
Wrong. Here’s an answer from AI (I asked for it to be understandable for someone with a 75 IQ, like you):
Prompt: Is it possible to receive backpay when granted Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI)?

Answer: Yes. When Social Security approves SSDI, it may owe you money for earlier months when you qualified but had not yet been paid. This money is called backpay.

Usually:

* Social Security does not pay for the first five full months after your disability started.
* Payments can begin with the sixth full month.
* SSDI may also cover up to 12 months before you applied, depending on when your disability began.

Example: Social Security decides that your payable benefits should have started 20 months ago. It may give you those 20 missed monthly payments as one backpay payment, minus any lawyer’s fee or other required deductions.

62498810
>>62498782
No you're dumb.

62498858
>>62498782
yeah i'm sure they handed you half a decade worth of "backpay"

62498860
>>62498458
can you give a qrd on your disability and how you got it and if its someting you an teach other anons like me? ive been NEET for the last 6 years but have no income itd be nice to get something back from all the taxes i paid for the 2 decades of wagecucking i did

62498875
>>62498860
>can you teach me how to be disabled?
i don't think that's how it works bud
good news though if you're stupid enough to ask a question like that then you're at least mentally disabled

62498969
>>62498810
>I have no argument so I’ll just say “no” and call him a name, like a literal child
Kek.
>>62498858
Here’s an AI response for you:
It happens more often than people realize. If someone’s claim takes years because of appeals, Social Security can award benefits retroactively to the established onset date (subject to the statutory rules). The back pay is paid as a lump sum once the claim is finally approved. Five years is uncommon, but it’s absolutely possible in cases with long delays.
>>62498860
> ive been NEET for the last 6 years but have no income itd be nice to get something back from all the taxes i paid for the 2 decades of wagecucking i did
You’re running the risk of applying too far from the time you worked. Just read up on everything there is to know about SSDI (pay attention to Date Last Insured, in your case). Make sure you fill out the application perfectly. Have a few grand set aside for expert opinions. When SSA denies you, hire a good SSDI lawyer. And wait.

62499151
>>62498586
Lol, relax schitzo. Its a larp

62499255
why is it so easy to apply for retardbux. i guess you can walk you can talk you can use a computer wtf where is you in need for tardbux? you can do everything a normal person can do so how you get extra care in form of money? autism is just a diagnosis your doc gives you and then? why one gets benefits for being fit for wageslaving i dont get it

62499279
>>62499255
Boomers and socialists. Just stop paying taxes. Go full independent and never declare an income ever again; the boomer ponziconomy is on its way out, it has been factually bankrupt for 40 years anyway, the only thing keeping it afloat is cashflow from wagies getting automatically extorted on their paycheck.

62499533
>>62498969
I'd rather not waste the tokens.

62499546
>>62498622
you can. my friend is on shizobux. when we was granted disability, he received a few years of back pay... and then his lawyer took a 30% cut.

but the amounts the OP is quoting is total nonsense. he's probably getting SSI, which maxes out at ~$1k/mo. https://www.ssa.gov/ssi/amount

62500088
>>62499546
> but the amounts the OP is quoting is total nonsense. he's probably getting SSI, which maxes out at ~$1k/mo. https://www.ssa.gov/ssi/amount
Can you read? I am getting SSDI, which is based on work history. SSI is for people whose work history is lacking.

62500188
>>62500088
i kneel, disability-sama.

62501405
>>62498458
>no means testing for income
Your tax return is how they know what you're up to.
Good News! Rental income is considered non-earned income for SSDI purposes

62501420
>NeetBux
>SchizoBux
>RetardBUx
>buxBuxBUx

sHUT THE FUCK UP

OP

>>62483359

>>62491183

>>62491495

>>62483350
>Buy SPCX

62491889
>>62483350

everyone should wait about 6 months, then decide.


way too early now. lots of down comin

62492490
>>62491495

62492529
>>62483350

62492905
>>62483350
If it drops below 100 I'll consider it.

62492909
>>62491495
Floor bros....the floor keeps lowering

62493017
>how did you arrive at that price target

62493049
>>62492529
That's why it went public. To sell the AI top, and get CAPEX moneis. He needed to piggyback xAI on two hyper successful, wide moat businesses.

62493824
>>62490969
It's probably the other way around. Terrestrial networks will cover the bulk of the market, and satellite will likely remain a niche market for wherever terrestrial coverage is poor.

Laser interconnections in the vacuum of space are lower latency due to the higher speed of light in vacuum, although hollow core fiber mostly negates that advantage

62493846
>>62493049
What's the wide moat of Starlink? Competitors are already launching. The competitors will have fewer satellites, which means they will be able to serve fewer customers per square kilometer, but that doesn't really matter much, because the bulk of the space internet market is low population density regions.

62494909
>>62492909
>>62492905
>>62492490
I'm telling you. BUY SPCX OR YOU'LL REGRET IT!

62495185
>>62483350
>$135 is the absolute floor
any updates?

62495365
>>62495185

62496083
>>62494909
>>62495365

62497248
>>62483350

62497262
I would make you fat fags buy my bags if I could.

62497303

62497308
>>62487453

They’ve been saying this stupid shit since 2001. Are you a fucken zoomer or something? If the rural areas do need it, how the fuck can they afford starlink which is $300/mo. You stupid fucken double nigger

62497323

62498562

62499311

62499315

62499329

62500590

62501206

item.title: Who's still buying BTC at this level? — 71 replies — 21.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-07-20 04:03 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62489527
>>62483295

62489716
>>62475235

62490257
>>62488664

62490309
>>62488156
not 39k? Lol

62491802

62491816
Anything around the 200 week moving average is a bargain.

I'm DCAing heavily at these levels. I want to get rid of all my cash by October.

62492766
>>62490257

62493410
>>62491816

62494059
>>62492766

62494064
>>62493410

62494358
>>62475235
I already have a decent bitcoin position and will be dca'ing more whenever it's below the 200 week. It's lame and boring but it works. My guess is that it bottoms between the 200 and 400 week MAs.

>>62475258
Dunk on ben all you want he's mostly right most of the time.

62494434
>>62494358
I'm not dunking, his videos are great.

62494839
>>62494358
Boring works. If you want to do more with your BTC, native staking already exists. TBVs look worth checking out once they're live.

62494846
>>62490309
It's easy to throw out price targets. Explaining why bitcoin will actually get there is the hard part.

62495219
>>62494434

62495227
>>62489716
What are you thinking there joe

62495280

62495641
>>62480931
buy 2 btc

62495924
>>62475235

>>62475971

62496666
>>62495924
you sure about that? it might go zero ?

62496951
>>62495641

62497289
>>62486528
great job man

62497294
Its very interesting to see the current transition of this board from being a crypto board to becoming a stock market board.
More and more anons slowly realizing crypto potential gains just arent there anymore and we might even be in a secular bear market.
If stock start crashing, crypto will have the biggest bear that it has ever seen.

62498541
>>62497294
It's the best time to accumulate then

62498999
>>62475235
bullish

62500137
>>62491816
How do you do that?

62500294
>>62500137
You can check the 200 week moving average on trading view, we are currently holding it as support. Any purchases below that average have given great returns.

62500310
>>62475235
Checked out sitting on mostly cash with about 20% in it right now.
Setting a significant buy order at 55k, if it drops a lot more I'll FOMO whatever I can otw down.
If that doesn't happen, I'll start DCAing again in mid/late September and begin incrementing the DCA in Nov.
If none of this makes sense, who cares, this is what my retard brain is telling me is a good plan.

62501164
>>62500294
Still not a guarantee

62501169
>>62475258
Never forget that Ben took his sexy assistant to Michael Saylor's 100k party, and not his wife.

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK? — 119 replies — 15.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?

Open thread • Replies: 119 • Updated: 2026-07-19 23:52 UTC

OP

Every time I see the UK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The FTSE is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the UK. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

Early replies (first 3)

62481973
>>62481958
the problem is that its being filled with immigrants who are living off tax payers money
thats the gist of why everyone is so pissed off here

62481986
>>62481973
So just right wing racebaiting?

No, I think it runs deeper than that. Most people have not personally had negative experiences with migrants, and reading the Daily Mail does not count.

I am trying to angle from a financial perspective too. People talk about the UK like it is a poor country but it is one of the richest countries on the planet. I think perhaps they are just spoiled and do not know what poor countries are really like. I blame social media.

62481994
>>62481958
>fantastic healthcare for minimal cost
my nan lived in birmingham and she came down with leukaemia and was gone within a couple of months. She never even got to see a doctor because the muslim immigrants were getting seen first. if i had something to lose i wouldnt even dare post this for fear of legal reprimand.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62490838
Sometimes I feel like responding to Europoors. Like I live in a capital city just minutes on foot to restaurants, shops, grocery stores. I pay less than 1k USD for my apartment. I can walk to work. I feel 100 percent safe, and could carry a firearm or knife if I so pleased but I am not a pussy. I often think about replying to Europoors and then I remember this meme.

62490850
>>62490585
Great post. A brit brangs about being able to walk/cycle outside and not getting stabbed.

62492082
>>62490850
bitter

62492388
>>62492082
Bitter about what?

62492412
>>62487400
>pictured: the entire UK fleet right before being crippled by their annual necessary repairs

62492424
>>62481973
>here
You need to get out before youre arrested for posting that

62492526
>>62492388
In context of the thread I was hardly bragging. I dare you to post where you live so we can have a go too

62492845
>>62492526
I live in a very expensive area created by corrupt political nepo insiders for corrupt political nepo insiders. Everybody has 2-3 newest Audi or mercedes SUVs and several millions in the bank. All the houses are 3,000+ square feet and have gardens. But not me, I'm an outsider here, and everybody knows that because they all know each other and that I'm not part of their club. All the neighbors are professional dogshit cunt parasites. So yeah you live in a better place.

62492892
>>62490838
>and could carry a firearm or knife

Love it how you're so compelled to mention this but are too afraid to use them for real because you'll be locked up.

Feel free to use it in a school or mall though. Seems like the average American pastime.

62492960
>>62492845
why would you move there? Sounds stupid

62493072
>>62481958
>Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs.
You can neither walk nor talk safely.
It's a beautiful country, it WAS a nice culture otherwise.
Now, if you have a daughter, or are a fuckable woman, you will get raped if not tortured by some gang, if you complain you will be arrested for racism, if you protest you will get beaten by the same worthless bobbies who refused to help, and it's all part of the meanest large scale society destuction, ethnic replacement, power grab and money grab back to feudalism ever in European history.
The economy is not better.

62493208
>>62492960
Because I'm a poorfag retard, it's not my place and I can live here for free.

62493518
>>62493072
what a load of tripe. 'Pakistani grooming gangs' weren't actually abducting and raping women you stupid retard. They were grooming them

62493619
>>62493518
even if that isn't a lie, "oh jeez, that's so much better, thanks!". you fucking clown, kys

62493666
>>62481958
if it's so great, stay there. stop running around and shitting everywhere. you flag is gay, and youor people are ugly af

62493751
>>62490585
>97% white
If you think it will stay that way in the next 20 years, you're delusional.
>>62482110

62493775
>>62481958
Because it run by Israel and occupied by Palestine

62493802
>>62481958
>Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?
out of the starmer frying pan into the burnham fire

62493836
>>62481958

62493843
>>62493751
do you think where you live will stay the same for 20 years too?

62493991
>>62493843
No, but that's completely beside the point because I wasn't the one bragging about living in some ethno state paradise.

62494575
>>62482009
This is an unacceptable post and the police have been informed.

62494624
Slimes abducted, raped, tortured and had dogs rape 250k British girls. If you think this is ok, you are a pedo globalist, a Jew, or a pedo globalist Jew.

62495552
>>62493802
>46 years of capitalist exploitation
>widely accepted that the country has declined since then
>somehow, this is not capitalisms fault
>in fact, it's socialisms fault
>somehow
>even though the nordic countries have done really well under socialism
>and america is a warzone full of drugs and homeless people
Yes, what we need is MORE corporate exploitation. That wealth is just about to trickle down! We have been waiting since *checks notes* Reagan but it's just about to happen!

62495765
>>62481958
Uk is hell on earth. 2nd worst country in the world right after india

62495935
>>62482008
>>62482025
>>62484965
Lmao. The second the numbers look bad, suddenly metrics don't matter.

GDP per capita. Median income. Salaries. Productivity. They're all "bad metrics" now.

The nice thing about GDP, salary, height, BMI, IQ tests, or any other quantitative measure is that the number still exists whether it makes you seethe or not.

Our poorest state is richer than your entire country on a per-person basis. Your doctors earn what our gas station managers make. Kneel.

62497286
>>62483303
Proportionality. Whites are still the majority. As it goes down, it'll become less sustainable, until it implodes.

62497297
>>62495552
>>even though the nordic countries have done really well under socialism
In what world are the nordic countries socialist? Are you retarded?

62497683
>>62481986
your country is being taken over by muslims. your women are raped en masse and you're worried about "right wingers". You are a weak man.

62500631
>>62497683
Last time right wingers took over a major country the world erupted in flames and global war. Muslims have never done that so yes I am more concerned about right wing extremism than a religion.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-20 06:03 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62500736

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHA GET FUCKED LONGS

62500780

62500801

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62500810

62500817
>>62500736
>>62500716
>>62500780
lame
gay

62500840
Insiders know, Clarity is being passed

62500863
>>62500840
That's what I speculate.

62500865
POGCHAMP

62500869
Polkadot is included in the eligible basket of assets for the T. Rowe Price Active Crypto ETF (TKNZ). The fund is an actively managed multi-token exchange-traded product, allowing its managers to rotate in and out of DOT along with other major cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum.

TDOT is an exchange-traded fund (ETF) that tracks the performance of Polkadot, allowing investors to gain exposure to the DOT cryptocurrency through traditional brokerage accounts without directly holding the digital asset.

Ticker TKNZ and ticker TDOT. What other Polkadot stocks are launching?

62500944
>+0.96%

Crypto cunts are buck broken

62500949
>>62500944
Kek it’s cracking me up

62501382
>>62500716
Check again!

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 142 replies — 7.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 142 • Updated: 2026-07-19 12:58 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496329
Oracle is active.
There are no coincidences.
This is a banking product.
For Banks.

Who here can find me the Everest episode of Chainlink Tonight?

Just want to confirm the Everest webpage linked to a Dept of Defense info page.

62496405
>>62496329
Oracle contract was lost. Bob already said as much when he alluded to certain partnerships not working out

62496425
>>62496405
He never said they lost Oracle contract you fucking retard.
To think he wouldn't prioritize the biggest software company in the world is fucking retarded and you are a fucken retard.

62496426
>>62496425
Just stop coping and be happy he got Mica. Oracle is dead and all evidence proves that

62496769
>>62496426
another nail to the evergay casket

62496941
Just want to confirm the Everest webpage linked to a Dept of Defense info page.

This has been asked for before and it’s long gone just like Jace/Bob taco meet up footage Bob wanted deleted. Lots of hush hush kek, probably nothing burger as nothing good Everest happens here

62497284
>>62461988
Piece of shit coin can’t even barely pump off mica

62497302
It will bull flag here next 5 years then make next move in 2032

62497334
This is the saddest thread in /biz/ right now. Just one or few bagholder samefagging to bump the thread, preaching about promised features & partners that cannot be verified.

Here's some verifiable facts:
- Everest preach about identity but that is basically as far as the "edit your profile" text fields in their wallet. It also took days to have the platform verify any identity document because they do this manually, probably by a freelancing jeet.
- Their facial recognition is now handled by Sumsub because their inhouse recognition was so ass that you could get recognized as someone else or locked out your wallet after a haircut.
- the only RWA Everest have so far is the GEMS thing that more or less Viarpee collaterizing a stupidly illiquid asset (their own emerald product which btw cannot compete with synthetic equivalent)
- There's ZERO use of the ID token right now outside the fabled "B2B partner". They said ID used as gas, but if you do try to move ID from Everchain to Ethereum it actually used ETH as gas, and it doesn't use ID for gas during staking in Everchain.
- ID token was supposed to be used for VNO but 5+ years after ICO, this bare minimum of the utility is nowhere to be found.
- Their fiat on-ramp could work one day and breaks the next hour without notice. The rates also not very attractive once you compare it with on ramp provider available through metamask, etc. Also nobody use their on-ramp widget other than in Everest wallet itself.
- You can't even send assets in their wallet without editing URL.
- You won't see or find anyone other than Bob working on Everest. Maybe Brad, but he was last seen years ago.
- Their newsletter which released every 6 months, barely feature verifiable data or quantifiable achievement.

At the very least linkies and xerpies already amass significantly more hodler to keep their thread organic. Overest has always pretend to be as 'useful' as Link which is pathetic given their current state.

62497358
>>62496941
Thank you.

62497362
>>62497334
1 ID = 1 LINK

62497396
>>62497362
Typical ever cuck cope

62497635
Retard they have an enterprise wallet for clients. This post sounds like someone who left after 2024 and is not paying attention to any details

62497666
>>62497635
Good job changing id saar so good

62497753
Hopefully Bob puts me out of my misery one day. I hate managing a daycare full of autistics who think they know everything bc token holder wallet is dog shit. Bob said it last year they don’t have bandwidth for perfect consumer wallet and enterprise wallet so teams time and energy goes into enterprise wallet until they get bigger. So yes it all works, just not for your bitch asses

62497850
>>62497753
Bro just stop coping and put your cuck cage on

62497867
I’m sick of the cuck cage, I want out of this matrix and Everest will do it. All the old bitter holders that took 6 figure losses and clearly have not moved on since they are commenting in here can go fuck themselves

62497871
Idk how to reply directly to someone but to the person shitting on consumer wallet: you would not be here commenting on Everest 4chan post if you had any bit of success in crypto. Whatever you find next will also rugpull on you until you sell then take off just like ID well

62497958
>>62497867
You do understand that everyone sees your 4ch ID, right? Right???

62497995
Yes you stupid goy I am nWDGiV1T. I’m sorry but you guys are pathetic fudders, try harder

62498030
>>62497958
>47 posts by that ID
KEK how heavy you think that fucks bags are

62498225
>>62498030
The heaviest bag. From his writing style, I know this anon is this one 'animal' user from everest telegram who bought since ICO, held through ATH, bought the token every month despite the chart & project silence, and diligently stake everytime even after knowing that stake rewards never ever outpace the price decline, and never sell.

He is not a whale, but his bag definitely cost the most. It was funny to see his unwavering belief and occasional tantrum, but now I can only feel pity.

62498287
>>62461988
they will all get MICA in the end because they need to tax everything

62498311
Is that why Binance could not get it and a shit ton of other coins that preached compliance. Sure thing brother, everyone gonna get it in the end for taxed. Dumb ass MiCA 2.0 is even stricter, it’s not getting easier from here idiot hahahahhaa. Most of Defi will be wiped out or forced to use licensed payment solutions

62498556
Sipping on some woodford reserve because I know I will have the last laugh here. Nobody laughs at me and calls it a day, I will be right in the end. Can’t wait to revisit this thread in 2030, have a nice night all

62498568
>>62498556
You will be bald by then retard

62498575
I actually do not stress and try to meditate/energy harvest for ID token so my full set of hair will still be around once bull flag is complete end of 2030

62498583
I am the whale, and I am a very nice whale with diamond hands. Any other new whales are more than welcome here, 1.4m mc with MiCA CASP to operate in Europe. They will be attacking DTCC settlements with that Kick Start Award from Stellar as the in. 99.5% down from ATH, I’m literally doing you idiots a favor and NOBODY is doing an inch of research and just calling me a bag holding retard instead. Do you know how fucking frustrating that feels to me?? Search Everest ID on Twitter and look at Teams posts from last 5 years, that is good start. This project is over most people’s heads, it takes too much time to fully understand it so the retards chase memes instead. One day, the Yin Yang will balance out in crypto too

62499388
>>62498583
its time to sell

62499407
My brother in Christ, my target is many dollars, not 1 cent

item.title: Most notable reddit investment factions — 28 replies — 7.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Most notable reddit investment factions

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-07-20 03:27 UTC

OP

I have lurked in reddit for like 3 days and i found 3 notable investment factions that hate each other.

1. WSB - you already know this homos.
2. Bogleheads - Boomers and millenials, won't call them retarded.
3. Dividend gangs
Interesting people. They hate 1 and 2.
They think buying voo is gambling and is similar to bitcoins because they rely on future price changing.
They want to own stale on free cahsflow and get bennies,
and reinvest bennies to get more bennies.
They say if you need to sell your stonks after you retire you are wrong.

How do you guys think about 3?
They seem kind of heterodox.

Early replies (first 3)

62496098
>>62496092
>2. Bogleheads - Boomers and millenials, won't call them retarded.
They're retarded also when we say millennials who the fuck are we talking about, because this board used to be 90% millennials gambling on crypto in 2017. Do we mean "millennial" (born 1981) or millennial (born 1993)?

62496113
>>62496098
Their bogle people seem to have more older millenials, but not sure.
I think they also have some gen zs as some people keep asking what to do with their small wages.

62496115
>>62496113
>le career progression meme
I'm out.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496714
cash crew #1
everyone hates us
CASH CREW REPRESENT

62496720
>>62496714
Fuck yeah bro lets hijack this thead for the cash crew

62496816
i go to plebbit normie financial advice reddits and be the only only one telling these ETF idiots to buy gold and silver (a good safe too which in itself is a good investement). They sperg, mald and cope everytime.

62497237
>>62496092
3 sounds absolutely retarded low T and risk adverse. If you think VOO is too risky you need to blast tren to save your nutless bag between your legs. You also have to consider what the alternative to NOT investing is, which is sitting in fiat, which is destined to go down no matter what from our money printing usurious economic system. So not only are they low T, risk adverse losers, they are also retarded.

62497244
>>62496816
>saaar pls buy my bags at cyclical decade highs saaaaaaaaaar
Indian housewife detected.

62497249
>>62496092
>They think buying voo is gambling and is similar to bitcoins because they rely on future price changing.

This is dumb. VOO goes up because it creates actual wealth. Not so for BTC.

62497259
>>62497249
VOO goes up because the stocks go up, which has little correlation with their revenue and even less with their actual valuation as companies. They go up because dollar go down. Just like Gold and Silver too.

62497271
>>62496092
>dividends reinvested are retarded

62497273
>>62496092
3 are essentially communists of the market.

1 and 2 are trying to find the underlying pattern of human behavior/investment so they at least have my respect for that.

62497338
>>62497271
Why? If you don't need the money and you want maximum growth, it makes sense.

62497357
>>62496714
Ayo!

62497367
>>62496092
I still dont know what a boglehead is. Is it just DCA into index funds? Typically these people win out long term, statistically.

62497369
>>62496092
>Dividend gangs
These people are 2 digit IQ retards that are math challenged. Basically, if stock A is $100 and will go to $110 in a year and appreciate 10% thereafter, but stock B is $100 and pays $2 (2%) in dividends a year and appreciates 5% annually, dividend retards will take stock B.

To them the idea of having shares and getting more shares and not selling the shares and the shares just give them dividends that is free money is somehow better than shares of stock that is ultimately worth more and they just sell a few shares of, because eventually one will run out of shares. Pretty much boy math if there ever was one.

62497372
>>62496816
Sir, every investment forum has been malding at me for 5 years recommending metals. They think their decades high 2-3% yield is somehow a much safer play. They have no idea $5k is still very cheap. Their point of reference over the last 50 years is about to leave them in tears

62497373
>>62496092
It's because if you do #2 the second you stop working / accumulating you have to start selling and it will eventually eat away at everything, so you have to be very strict only 4% max and even that can rekt you.
#3 seems like you can get the best of both worlds, you preserve ownership and live off the cash flow, but it's all the same shit in the end.
Or is it, preserving ownership is pretty powerful as opposed to the mathematical certainty of eventually losing ownership.
I wish reit income were taxes like ordinary dividends.

62497376
>>62497372
If youre young you should not be buying gold, or betting on silver. Save that for when youre 70

62497387
>>62497376
I am glad I didn’t listen to this advice 5 years ago. I’m glad I won’t be listening to this advice in the future. Metals are undergoing a once in a generation remonitization and it’s the comfiest and easiest hold than any paper

62497395
>>62497387
Dude, I own silver. I just dont put everything into it because you genuinely shouldnt do that.

62497397
>>62497338
QED.

62497416
>>62496092
bogling can be good for growth investment, but the goal should always be to have as much non-sale income as possible. if you're not going to have enough income then start selling shares but otherwise keep them.
starting with divvies has its own advantages especially if you get laid off.

62497433
>>62497369

>>62497373
Retard in point

62497502
>>62496098
How is 81 a "millennial"? That's where millennial started and its been that way from the beginning

62497612
>>62497433
Well refute the point faggot. You can't

62498469
>>62497612
>you have to start selling and it will eventually eat away at everything

Yeah there's the short bus, the feeling that a dividend is eternal and therefore better to have beyond the sheer expected value.

62501118
I used to be dividend gang but that was retarded. Have gone full boglehead have 20 years left until retirement but I am aimed to be at 3.2 million all in. ROTH when I hit 60.

Just going to live off the 2% (64k a year) and let it grow from 60 to 70. At 70 my bond ladder hits along with my upping from 2% to 4% along with social security turns my income up to anywhere from 160k to 210k a year depending on the market.

item.title: ecash — 22 replies — 5.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
ecash

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-20 03:38 UTC

OP

I'm only going to post this once, for two reasons

1. I feel a duty to inform you fags given I've been lurking this board for so long.
2. I want to be able to point back at this post forever, the first eCash alpha, for pure self pleasure.

Bitcoin is hardforking on the 22 aug. Not the 110 bullshit, but eCash, a continuation of the Bitcoin project without all the gatekeeping and baggage. You havent heard of it because its not loud, its the quiet next move for the money that matters.

For the newfags I'll break it down simply. Bitcoin has the ability to do softforks, a 100% safe, backwards compatibility, opt-in, ignorable upgrades that Satoshi left the ability for. The reasons that they arent used is a simple fact that Bitcoins leadership has been compromised, and its mindshare is being wasted on purposeful dead ends like Lightning and ARK. Simple opcodes like OP-VAULT, OP-CAT, and BIP-300 & 301 havent been merged and shit like Taproot has is insane. The focus is to redirect fees to corporations on top instead of the network itself.

eCash is an exact fork of Bitcoin Core, with only softfork being added. Being always compatible with Bitcoin Core, inheriting all the future work for free. BIP-300 & 301 softforks are initially added to allow for Drive Chains. Merge mined Sidechains for Bitcoin that enable Bitcoin to actually function and Hyper Bitcoinization to actually happen, while preserving the security model, and keeping the base chain easily verifiable.

Future generations will see Bitcoin as V1 of something that became V2: eCash on August 22nd. The realization will be slow and painful, but extremely profitable for those who know. Its necessary that the Bitcoin project continue, with or without the name

Early replies (first 3)

62499074
Your thread answered my question about which Bitcoin continuation projects should I dumped my hard earned money on next? I was in the dark but now I see the light. Thank you faggot.

62499078
>>62499074
Its the continuation because unlike every other scam you're getting 1:1 your Bitcoin holdings out of 21m, but it actually does things. It will beat Bitcoin, youre welcome, fag

62499082
>>62499072
I'm going to mine it like a total pig with my BitAxe 601.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62499097
>>62499078
This very good bitcoin saar

62499106
It's 2026 you stupid asshole, no one cares about crypto anymore.

62499396
>>62499072
I thought eCash (XEC) forked years ago? Amaury led the development. What the heck, is there another ecash now?

62499409
An ecash just flew over my house. I'll put some money on it though. Thanks for the hot tip.

62499549
>>62499072
>eCash is an exact fork of Bitcoin Core, with only softfork being added
No, it's a hard fork that includes "manually reassigning" Satoshi's coins for the eCash developers and investors. But you're right Bitcoin is fucked from development perspective and needs a hard fork to move forward. At the same time it should future proof its chain security by adding tail issuance (break meme 21M promise) or switch to PoS, and maybe add other non-Bitcoin functionality that's hard to add later. Forking Bitcoin is still one of the the best ways to distribute supply fairly, but this absolutely shouldn't include stealing other people's coins, even if they've been sitting dormant for a long time.

62499578
>>62499549
How do you determine which coins are satoshi's? Sure the first block rewards are his but that's nowhere near 500k tokens. So are we just taking unspent coins that may or may not be satoshi's coins?

Distributing supply fairly is not desirable or valuable in this context.

62499590
>>62499578
>How do you determine which coins are satoshi's?
Google "Patoshi pattern". When people talk about Satoshi's coins they are referring to these 1.1M BTC that have this unique "mining fingerprint".
>So are we just taking unspent coins that may or may not be satoshi's coins?
Yes.
>Distributing supply fairly is not desirable or valuable in this context.
Of course distributing coin supply fairly is desirable when a new chain launches.

62499719
>>62499578
>Distributing supply fairly is not desirable or valuable in this context.

The context is they mined 1.1 million coins, like the other anon said, google the Patoshi Pattern.

Satoshi will now have 1.1 million Bitcoin + 550k ecash. Their wallets actually have more coins than before the fork. We all know that Bitcoin is never moving though, it was never intended to. A small price to pay for saving every other users money, and moving again towards the Bitcoin endgame.

62499725
>>62499074
samefagging jeet

62499738
>>62499549
>At the same time it should future proof its chain security by adding tail issuance (break meme 21M promise) or switch to PoS

Adding 300 & 301 softforks are the only real solution to NOT break the 21m promise. With Drivechains enabled, miners get all the fees from any number of chains, all using the same base unit. These fees will start replacing the block rewards as they continue to halve away.

This was all supposed to already have been implemented on BTC a decade ago, but as I said its compromised and nothing useful will be merged.

62499748
Just buy monero lol

62499767
>>62499719
The better approach would've been to mint new coins instead of stealing old ones. Breaking the 21M meme supply cap is more neutral than choosing Satoshi to be a victim of theft.

62499781
>>62499767
How is it theft if the Satoshi wallet still has 1.1m Bitcoin?

62499791
>>62499781
It's theft on ecash chain. Satoshi wallets get different treatment compared to other wallets. They could've taken a 2% tax from all wallets instead. Or just print new supply (same outcome with direct tax vs. tax through inflation).

62499976
BSV solves the shit coin problem

62500729
>>62499072
Whats the plan to defend the network against 51% attacks? Looks to be that the 6+ chains will be merge mined but they compete with all forks for hashrate. Do they think people will stop mining btc after the bip110 passes?

62500740
>>62499072
>You havent heard of it because its not loud, its the quiet next move for the money that matters.
There was a thread about it a couple of weeks ago. We where talking about ways to dump it for more btc.

62500761
that's disappointing that existing bitcoin will be grandfathered in, I thought they were going to start from scratch

62501132
>>62500729
who says BIP-110 will pass? Saylor is taking a hard stance against it
